articles.title: LVMH (ENXTPA:MC) Stock Looks Fairly Valued On Its 38% Slump
articles.summary: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton Société Européenne is in an interesting spot: after the share price decline of around 38% over the past 3 years, the current checks suggest the stock now trades close to its estimated intrinsic value using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) approach and around fair on earnings based multiples, yet it still carries a low overall value score. Over the past 3 years, LVMH's share price has declined about 37.7%, which means recent holders have seen meaningful value...

articles.title: Richemont Surges After 20% Sales Growth Crushes 11% Forecast
articles.summary: Strong jewelry demand drove quarterly sales well above expectations, sending Richemont shares to a record high.

articles.title: Cartier-owner Richemont's sales beat boosts luxury sector
articles.summary: STORY: Jewellery appears to be shining bright. Cartier-owner Richemont gave a boost to the luxury sector on Wednesday after comfortably beating its Q1 sales forecast. The Swiss-based group, which also owns brands Piaget and Van Cleef & Arpels, saw accelerating demand for its jewellery and watches. And said its sales rose by a fifth to $7.24 billion in the three months to the end of June. The figure soared past analyst forecasts, with one branding it a “flabbergasting” result. Richemont's jewellery business in particular saw sales rise by close to a quarter, much better than the 13.5% forecasts. The result sent Richemont's shares 6% higher in early trading - while the Stoxx European Luxury 10 index, which includes industry giants like LVMH and Hermes, rose 2.5%. Investors were encouraged by the group’s positive comments on the greater China region, where sales rose at a double-digit rate. Another analyst said Richemont was benefiting from growth at both ends of the luxury market… With demand for high-end jewellery among the ultra-wealthy and value-for-money products among aspirational consumers.
